stories.title: Red Dot winner Yanice Yang advances AI-powered health technology
stories.simple_summary: A person named Yanice Yang won an award for creating an AI health technology tool.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly a news item about a specific person's achievement — nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Harry Potter publisher to receive millions in Anthropic copyright settlement
stories.simple_summary: The publisher of Harry Potter books is getting paid money by an AI company called Anthropic because the company used Harry Potter books to train its AI without permission.
stories.why_it_matters: This sets a precedent that authors and publishers can be paid when their work is used to train AI — good news if you've written anything or own content.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: There are other internet browsers besides Chrome and Safari that you can use instead.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're unhappy with Chrome or Safari, you have alternatives available — but this mostly matters to people interested in exploring new tools.

stories.title: As China advances in AI, Trump faces a new test in the technology race
stories.simple_summary: China is getting better at AI technology, which is a concern for the United States government.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about global politics and business competition between countries — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
stories.simple_summary: A company called Passionfroot that helps businesses and content creators connect with each other just raised $15 million to expand to the US.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and content creators looking for work — not relevant to most small business owners.

stories.title: Artificial intelligence will drive an unprecedented natural-gas deficit, this investor warns. Here are the stocks to buy before the crunch.
stories.simple_summary: An investor is warning that AI will use a lot more natural gas electricity, and suggesting certain stocks to buy.
stories.why_it_matters: This is investment advice — you should not buy stocks based on a headline, and would need to talk to a financial advisor.

stories.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook's company) created a watermarking system (an invisible label) to mark images that its AI creates, so people can tell AI images from real photos.
stories.why_it_matters: In the future, you might be able to tell whether images online are fake AI creations, which helps you spot misleading content.

stories.title: Utility companies promise to spare us from AI’s energy bill
stories.simple_summary: US electricity companies are promising not to raise your power bills too much because of all the energy that AI systems are using.
stories.why_it_matters: Your electricity company is committing to controlling costs as AI grows — good news for your future bills, though this is still a developing issue.

stories.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
stories.simple_summary: A new company called Glow is launching a security tool to protect businesses from risks caused by the widespread use of AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This matters mainly to large businesses concerned about protecting their computers and data — not something a small business needs to worry about yet.

stories.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
stories.simple_summary: A company called Synthesia is letting employees practice conversations with AI characters that look like real people, for training purposes.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a new training tool for large companies — not something that affects most small business owners right now.

stories.title: Will your job be replaced by AI? Here are the roles most affected
stories.simple_summary: The BBC published information about which jobs are most likely to be affected by AI.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're worried about your job, this headline suggests a useful story to read — but the actual impact will depend on your specific work.

stories.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
stories.simple_summary: There is a rumor on social media about whether one AI company might buy another AI company.
stories.why_it_matters: This is rumors among investors and tech insiders — nothing that affects you right now.
